posts.title: Welcome to Jeremy Gallery
posts.excerpt: A short introduction to the Jeremy Gallery mobile app experience.
posts.content:
Welcome to Jeremy Gallery.

This mobile app is designed as a simple and focused way to browse photography collections, stories, and selected portfolio works.

The first version of the app focuses on Portfolio, including albums and photos loaded from a remote JSON file.

Blog will become a place for short notes, photo stories, travel impressions, and updates.

posts.title: Milano Moments
posts.excerpt: Light, streets, architecture and quiet details from Milano.
posts.content:
Milano is a city of structure, texture and small visual surprises.

The photographs in this collection focus less on landmarks and more on the feeling of walking through the city.

Details, shadows, reflections and quiet moments often become more memorable than the obvious views.

posts.title: Designing a Mobile Gallery
posts.excerpt: Thoughts behind building a clean mobile browsing experience for photography.
posts.content:
A mobile gallery should feel calm and easy to navigate.

The goal is not to overload the screen, but to allow each image and each album to breathe.

Navigation should be simple, visual hierarchy should be clear, and the user should always know where they are.

posts.title: Remote Blog JSON Test
posts.author: Jeremy
posts.excerpt: This post confirms that the app is loading Blog content from GitHub Pages.
